channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Black Hat USA
New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant&nbsp;ofmalware&nbsp;called Chaosthat'scapable of&nbsp;hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure. "Chaos malware is increasingly targeting misconfigured cloud deployments, expanding beyond its traditional focus on routers and edge devices,"&nbsp;Darktrace said in a new&nbsp;report.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Operationalizing AI Security Is the Next Great Enterprise Hurdle
item.description: <p>NWN launches an AI-powered security platform to tackle tool sprawl, alert fatigue, and modern cyber threats in the era of agentic enterprises.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-nwn-ai-security-platform-tool-sprawl-alert-fatigue/">Why Operationalizing AI Security Is the Next Great Enterprise Hurdle</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Zeus Kerravala

item.title: 10 ChatGPT AI Prompts L1 SOC Analysts Can Use in Their Daily Work
item.description: <p>Discover 10 practical ChatGPT prompts SOC analysts can use to speed up triage, analyze threats, improve documentation, and enhance incident response workflows.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chatgpt-prompts-soc-analysts-incident-response/">10 ChatGPT AI Prompts L1 SOC Analysts Can Use in Their Daily Work</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Masjesu Botnet Emerges as DDoS-for-Hire Service Targeting Global IoT Devices
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS)&nbsp;attacks. Called Masjesu, the botnet has been advertised via Telegram as a DDoS-for-hire service since it first surfaced in 2023. It's capable of targeting a wide range of IoT devices, such as routers and gateways, spanning multiple architectures. "Built for
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fraud Rockets Higher in Mobile-First Latin America
item.description: Cyber-fraudsters move quickly from compromised devices to account takeover to funds transfer, shifting money before many financial institutions can react.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Full Sail University to Open IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range on Campus
Niobium Introduces The Fog
Pluralsight Launches SecureReady to Help Organizations Build Job-Ready Cybersecurity Teams
APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
item.description: The Russian threat actor known&nbsp;as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite&nbsp;codenamed PRISMEX. "PRISMEX combines advanced steganography, component object model (COM) hijacking, and legitimate cloud service abuse for command-and-control," Trend Micro
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iranian Threat Actors Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure Via Exposed PLCs
item.description: Attackers compromised Internet-facing OT devices and caused file and display manipulation, operational disruption, and financial losses across sectors.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: New Scam Alert: QR Codes Replace Links in Traffic Ticket Phishing
item.description: <p>Scammers are using fake traffic violation texts with QR codes to steal personal and financial data, posing as state courts and government agencies.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-qr-code-traffic-ticket-scam-2026/">New Scam Alert: QR Codes Replace Links in Traffic Ticket Phishing</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: ‘BlueHammer’ Exploit Targets Windows, Potentially Impacting 1 Billion+ Devices
item.description: <p>A researcher released a working ‘BlueHammer’ Windows zero-day exploit that could impact over 1 billion devices, granting SYSTEM-level access and leaving no patch yet.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-bluehammer-windows-zero-day-exploit-microsoft/">‘BlueHammer’ Exploit Targets Windows, Potentially Impacting 1 Billion+ Devices</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing to Use AI to Find and Fix Critical Software Vulnerabilities
item.description: Anthropic launches Project Glasswing, using its Claude Mythos Preview AI to autonomously identify and fix undiscovered vulnerabilities in critical software

item.title: Python Supply-Chain Compromise
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: US Thwarts DNS Hijacking Network Controlled by Russian APT28 Hackers
item.description: The FBI deployed a method to unplug US-based routers compromised by APT28 from the threat actor’s malicious network

item.title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) company Anthropic announced a new cybersecurity initiative&nbsp;called Project&nbsp;Glasswing&nbsp;that&nbsp;will use a preview version of its new frontier model, Claude Mythos,&nbsp;to find and address security vulnerabilities. The model will&nbsp;be used by&nbsp;a small&nbsp;set of organizations, including Amazon Web Services, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike,&
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Claude Discovers Apache ActiveMQ Bug Hidden for 13 Years
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude AI has helped researchers find a vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Classic

item.title: Iran‑Backed Threat Actors Hit US CNI Providers via Internet‑Facing OT Assets
item.description: CISA has revealed Iranian attacks causing disruption and financial loss at US critical infrastructure firms

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known&nbsp;as Contagious&nbsp;Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's packages were designed to impersonate legitimate developer tooling [...], while quietly functioning as malware loaders, extending Contagious Interview’s established playbook into a coordinated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How to Comply with HECVAT in 2026 (Free Checklist) | UpGuard
item.description: If you're a vendor supporting the education sector, this checklist will help you prepare for any HECVAT questionnaires that come your way.

item.title: Why is HECVAT Important? Cybersecurity Risks in Education | UpGuard
item.description: Find out why schools should begin using HECVAT to monitor vendor security postures.

item.title: Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (Free Template) | UpGuard
item.description: Utilize this free questionnaire template to evaluate third-party vendors against the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit.

item.title: What is HECVAT? Protecting Students from Vendor Security Risks | UpGuard
item.description: A comprehensive overview of HECVAT 4. Learn how to leverage it to reduce your third-party risk exposure and likelihood of student data compromise.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), cybersecurity and intelligence&nbsp;agencies warned&nbsp;Tuesday. "These attacks have led to diminished PLC functionality, manipulation of display data and, in some cases, operational disruption and financial
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited N-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders that appear benign but return sensitive data to the attacker's server.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Trump’s Proposed CISA Cuts Spark Alarm Among Cybersecurity Experts
item.description: <p>Trump’s proposed budget cuts to CISA raise concerns about U.S. cyber defense, as experts warn of reduced collaboration and threat intelligence sharing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-trump-cisa-budget-cuts-2027/">Trump’s Proposed CISA Cuts Spark Alarm Among Cybersecurity Experts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Cybersecurity in the Age of Instant Software
item.description: <p>AI is rapidly changing how software is written, deployed, and used. Trends point to a future where AIs can write custom software quickly and easily: “instant software.” Taken to an extreme, it might become easier for a user to have an AI write an application on demand&#8212;a spreadsheet, for example&#8212;and delete it when you’re done using it than to buy one commercially. Future systems could include a mix: both traditional long-term software and ephemeral instant software that is constantly being written, deployed, modified, and deleted.</p> <p>AI is changing cybersecurity as well. In particular, AI systems are getting better at finding and patching vulnerabilities in code. This has implications for both attackers and defenders, depending on the ways this and related technologies improve...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description: The Russia-linked threat actor known&nbsp;as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard) has been linked to a new campaign that has compromised insecure MikroTik and TP-Link routers and modified their settings to turn them into malicious infrastructure under their control as part of a cyber espionage campaign since at least May&nbsp;2025. The large-scale exploitation campaign has&nbsp;been codenamed&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In&nbsp;the rapid evolution of&nbsp;the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security&nbsp;leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing. According to new research from&nbsp;the Ponemon&nbsp;Institute, hundreds of applications within the typical enterprise remain disconnected from centralized identity systems.&nbsp;These&nbsp;"dark
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New FBI Warning: Chinese Apps Could Expose User Data
item.description: <p>The FBI is warning Americans about data security risks tied to foreign-developed mobile apps, especially those linked to China.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fbi-warns-foreign-apps-data-security-risks/">New FBI Warning: Chinese Apps Could Expose User Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Russian APT28 Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Credentials, UK Security Agency Warns
item.description: Newly identified malicious campaigns are linked to virtual private servers modified by APT28 to operate as malicious DNS servers

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GPU Rowhammer Attack Enables Privilege Escalation and Full System Compromise
item.description: GPUBreach uses GPU Rowhammer on GDDR6 to flip bits, corrupt page tables and escalate to system root

item.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
item.description: Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek, Kelly Jackson Higgins

item.title: Human vs AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
item.description: As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi, Kristina Beek

item.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
item.description: A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results.
item.dc:creator: Joan Goodchild

item.title: GrafanaGhost Exploit Bypasses AI Guardrails for Silent Data Exfiltration
item.description: GrafanaGhost chains AI prompt injection and URL flaws to exfiltrate sensitive Grafana data

item.title: Focusing on the People in Cybersecurity at RSAC 2026 Conference
item.description: AI dominated RSAC 2026 Conference, but it's still the humans in cybersecurity who matter most.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Marks

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy&nbsp;botnet. "A purpose-built Python scanner continuously sweeps major cloud IP ranges for vulnerable targets, automatically installing malicious nodes&nbsp;via ComfyUI-Manager if no exploitable node is already
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over $17bn Lost to Cyber Fraud in the Last Year, Warns FBI
item.description: Cryptocurrency scams alone cost victims over $7 billion, while AI-enabled fraud threats are on the rise, says FBI

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Recurring Credential Incidents
item.description: When talking about credential security, the focus usually lands on breach prevention. This&nbsp;makes sense&nbsp;when IBM’s 2025 Cost of a Data Breach&nbsp;Report puts the average cost of a breach at $4.4&nbsp;million. Avoiding even one major incident is enough to justify most security investments, but that headline figure obscures the more persistent problems caused by recurring credential
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Storm-1175 Exploits Flaws in High-Velocity Medusa Attacks
item.description: Microsoft has released a new report about the Storm-1175 group and its connection to Medusa ransomware

item.title: Hong Kong Police Can Force You to Reveal Your Encryption Keys
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Fortinet Releases Emergency Patch After FortiClient EMS Bug Is Exploited
item.description: Fortinet has updated its FortiClient EMS product after zero-day attacks surfaced

item.title: As breakout time accelerates, prevention-first cybersecurity takes center stage
item.description: Threat actors are using AI to supercharge tried-and-tested TTPs. When attacks move this fast, cyber-defenders need to rethink their own strategy.

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take full control of a&nbsp;host. The efforts have been&nbsp;codenamed GPUBreach, GDDRHammer,&nbsp;and GeForge. GPUBreach goes a step further&nbsp;than GPUHammer, demonstrating for the first time that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Context Gap: How Nearly Half of Your Time is Lost to Investigation | UpGuard
item.description: Security teams waste 43% of their response time on manual context gathering, making high-context intelligence a necessity in modern cybersecurity.

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A&nbsp;China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and break into susceptible internet-facing&nbsp;systems. "The threat actor's high operational tempo and proficiency in identifying exposed perimeter assets have proven successful, with recent
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question&nbsp;is CVE-2025-59528 (CVSS score: 10.0), a code injection vulnerability that could result in remote code execution. "The CustomMCP node allows users to input configuration settings for connecting
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
item.description: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Axios Attack Shows Complex Social Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: New Mexico’s Meta Ruling and Encryption
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An&nbsp;Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid&nbsp;ongoing conflict in the Middle&nbsp;East. The&nbsp;activity, assessed to be ongoing, was carried out in three distinct attack waves that took place on March 3, March 13, and March 23, 2026, per Check&nbsp;Point. "The campaign is primarily
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea
item.description: Threat&nbsp;actors likely associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) have been observed using GitHub as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure in multi-stage attacks targeting organizations in South&nbsp;Korea. The&nbsp;attack chain,&nbsp;per Fortinet FortiGuard&nbsp;Labs, involves obfuscated Windows shortcut (LNK) files acting as the starting point to drop a decoy PDF
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Fortinet Flaw Allows Unauthorized Access to Enterprise Systems
item.description: <p>Fortinet warns of a critical FortiClient EMS zero-day vulnerability that is currently being exploited, allowing attackers to bypass authentication and execute commands.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fortinet-forticlient-ems-zero-day-active-exploitation/">New Fortinet Flaw Allows Unauthorized Access to Enterprise Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Apple Rolls Out Fix: New macOS Update Could Protect 100M Mac Users
item.description: <p>Apple’s macOS 26.4 update adds a Terminal warning to help stop ClickFix-style attacks by flagging potentially harmful pasted commands.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-macos-update-terminal-malware-warning-clickfix/">Apple Rolls Out Fix: New macOS Update Could Protect 100M Mac Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
item.description: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards groups recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no&nbsp;longer lives&nbsp;on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting&nbsp;it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, and mobile devices, taking advantage of the fact that many SOC&nbsp;workflows are still fragmented by&nbsp;platform.&nbsp; For security leaders, this creates&nbsp;a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How LiteLLM Turned Developer Machines Into Credential Vaults for Attackers
item.description: The&nbsp;most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That&nbsp;laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, build tools, and now local AI&nbsp;agents. In&nbsp;March 2026, the TeamPCP threat&nbsp;actor proved just how&nbsp;valuable developer&nbsp;machines are. Their&nbsp;supply chain attack on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Wants to Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography by 2029
item.description: <p>Google <a href="https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/safety-security/cryptography-migration-timeline/">says</a> that it will fully transition to post-quantum cryptography by 2029. I think this is a good move, not because I think we will have a useful quantum computer anywhere near that year, but because crypto-agility is always a good thing.</p> <p>Slashdot <a href="https://it.slashdot.org/story/26/03/27/2123239/google-moves-post-quantum-encryption-timeline-up-to-2029">thread</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
item.description: Threat actors associated&nbsp;with Qilin&nbsp;and Warlock ransomware operations&nbsp;have been&nbsp;observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver&nbsp;(BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised hosts, according to findings from Cisco Talos and Trend&nbsp;Micro. Qilin attacks analyzed by Talos&nbsp;have been&nbsp;found to deploy a malicious DLL named "msimg32.dll,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identities of two of the key figures associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation. One of the threat actors, who went by the alias UNKN, functioned as a representative of the group, advertising the ransomware in June 2019 on the XSS cybercrime forum
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: $285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
item.description: Drift&nbsp;has revealed that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to&nbsp;the theft of $285&nbsp;million was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) that began in the fall of&nbsp;2025. The&nbsp;Solana-based decentralized exchange described it as "an attack six months in the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent&nbsp;implant. "Every package contains three files (package.json, index.js, postinstall.js), has no description, repository,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
item.description: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the&nbsp;wild. The&nbsp;vulnerability, tracked&nbsp;as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS score: 9.1), has been described as a pre-authentication API access bypass leading to privilege escalation. "An improper access control vulnerability [CWE-284] in FortiClient EMS may allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Inconsistent Privacy Labels Don't Tell Users What They Are Getting
item.description: Data privacy labels are a great idea for mobile apps, but the current versions just aren't good enough.
item.dc:creator: Bree Fowler

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025,&nbsp;following a two-year&nbsp;period of minimal targeting in the&nbsp;region. The campaign has been attributed&nbsp;to TA416, a cluster of activity that overlaps with DarkPeony, RedDelta, Red Lich, SmugX, UNC6384, and Vertigo&nbsp;Panda. "This TA416 activity included multiple
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
item.description: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Android Alert: 50 Google Play Apps Linked to ‘NoVoice’ Malware Reached 2.3M Downloads
item.description: <p>NoVoice malware was found in 50 Android apps on Google Play, with 2.3 million downloads, by bypassing detection and targeting outdated devices.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-novoice-malware-android-google-play-50-apps/">Android Alert: 50 Google Play Apps Linked to ‘NoVoice’ Malware Reached 2.3M Downloads</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: FBI Declares Surveillance System Breach a ‘Major Incident’
item.description: <p>China-linked hackers breached an FBI surveillance system, exposing sensitive investigation data and prompting a “major incident” classification.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-china-linked-fbi-surveillance-breach-major-incident/">FBI Declares Surveillance System Breach a ‘Major Incident’</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat&nbsp;actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft Defender Security Research&nbsp;Team. "Instead of exposing command execution through URL parameters or request bodies, these web shells rely on threat actor-supplied cookie values to gate execution,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Toast, Inc.
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 30–April 3.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-breakthroughs-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Breakthroughs, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
item.description: &quot;Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs&quot; can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
item.description: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open source artifacts across containers, libraries, agent skills, and GitHub Actions.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
item.description: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Company that Secretly Records and Publishes Zoom Meetings
item.description: <p>WebinarTV searches the internet for public Zoom invites, joins the meetings, secretly records them, and <a href="https://www.404media.co/this-company-is-secretly-turning-your-zoom-calls-into-ai-podcasts/">publishes</a> (alternate <a href="https://archive.ph/TYd4L">link</a>) the recordings. It doesn&#8217;t use the Zoom record feature, so Zoom can&#8217;t do anything about it.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The&nbsp;maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracked&nbsp;as UNC1069. Maintainer Jason Saayman said the attackers tailored their social engineering efforts "specifically to me" by first approaching him under the guise of the founder of a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
item.description: The next major&nbsp;breach hitting your clients probably won't come&nbsp;from inside their&nbsp;walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody in IT knows about. That's the new attack surface, and most organizations are underprepared for&nbsp;it. Cynomi's new&nbsp;guide, Securing the Modern Perimeter: The Rise of Third-Party
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers&nbsp;have discovered a new version of&nbsp;the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the&nbsp;trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile operating&nbsp;systems. The&nbsp;malware&nbsp;has&nbsp;been found to conceal itself within seemingly benign apps, such as enterprise messengers and food delivery services, while
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1,&nbsp;2026. "Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Drift Protocol through a novel attack involving durable nonces, resulting in a rapid takeover of Drift’s Security Council administrative powers," the&
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Phishing Platform Used in Credential Theft Campaigns Against C-Suite Execs
item.description: A large-scale credential theft campaign targeting senior executives has been linked to a previously unknown automated phishing platform called Venom

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bracken, Kristina Beek

item.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
item.description: The company's 8-K filing notes &quot;unauthorized access&quot; and that it has activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A&nbsp;large-scale credential harvesting operation&nbsp;has been&nbsp;observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secrets, shell command history, Stripe API keys, and GitHub tokens at&nbsp;scale. Cisco&nbsp;Talos has attributed the operation to a threat cluster it tracks&nbsp;as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future promise.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Patch Now: Chrome Flaw Under Active Attack, Google Confirms
item.description: <p>Google patches 21 Chrome vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw that could enable code execution and full device compromise.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-zero-day-cve-2026-5281-active-exploit/">Patch Now: Chrome Flaw Under Active Attack, Google Confirms</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier
